Class LobbedAttack

java.lang.Object
logic.unit.strategy.tactic.LobbedAttack
All Implemented Interfaces:
Describable, AttackStrategy
Direct Known Subclasses:
LobbedFireAttack
public class LobbedAttack extends Object implements AttackStrategy
Implements a high-arc combat strategy that bypasses environmental obstacles. Units using this strategy "lob" projectiles at a target location, allowing them to strike enemies behind walls or other units. However, this strategy suffers from a "dead zone" where it cannot target tiles immediately adjacent to the attacker.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from interface logic.unit.strategy.tactic.AttackStrategy

  • Constructor Details

    • LobbedAttack

      public LobbedAttack()
      Default constructor for initializing the Lobbed Attack. Sets the default minimum range to 2 tiles.

    • LobbedAttack

      public LobbedAttack(int minAttackRange)
      Parameterized constructor to define a custom minimum attack range.
      Parameters:
      minAttackRange - The minimum distance (inclusive) required to target a tile.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Retrieves the display name of this artillery-style tactic.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface Describable
      Returns:
      The string "Artillery".

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Retrieves the tooltip description explaining the lobbing and range constraints.
      Specified by:
      getDescription in interface Describable
      Returns:
      A string detailing the ability to ignore obstacles and the minimum range restriction.

    • getAttackableTiles

      public List<List<Tile>> getAttackableTiles(Tile attacker)
      Calculates the tiles that can be selected as the primary point of impact. Unlike LineAttack, this method does not stop when encountering obstacles; it calculates a valid window between minAttackRange and the unit's max range.
      Specified by:
      getAttackableTiles in interface AttackStrategy
      Parameters:
      attacker - The tile currently occupied by the unit initiating the attack.
      Returns:
      A nested List containing the center points of all valid lobbing targets.

    • getImpactTiles

      public List<List<Tile>> getImpactTiles(Tile attacker)
      Calculates the splash damage clusters for every potential lobbing target. Every valid center tile identified in targeting is expanded into a diamond-shaped Area of Effect (AoE) based on the attacker's impactRadius.
      Specified by:
      getImpactTiles in interface AttackStrategy
      Parameters:
      attacker - The tile occupied by the unit performing the attack.
      Returns:
      A nested List where each inner list represents the AoE cluster for a specific target point.

    • applyEffects

      public void applyEffects(Tile attacker, Tile target)
      Executes the combat logic for the lobbed projectile. Applies standard damage to any unit found at the impact location.
      Specified by:
      applyEffects in interface AttackStrategy
      Parameters:
      attacker - The tile occupied by the unit firing the artillery.
      target - The specific tile selected for the lobbed impact.

    • getMinAttackRange

      public int getMinAttackRange()
      Retrieves the minimum range required for the lobbing attack to function.
      Returns:
      The integer minimum range.

    • setMinAttackRange

      public void setMinAttackRange(int minAttackRange)
      Sets the minimum attack range. Enforces a floor value of 2 to ensure the "artillery" flavor is maintained.
      Parameters:
      minAttackRange - The value to set as the minimum targeting distance.

    • playSound

      public void playSound(String unitID, double volume)
      Triggers the specific audio feedback for this lobbed attack. Varies sound effects based on the unit type (e.g., Warships fire cannons, Sages use magic, and standard units use arrows).
      Specified by:
      playSound in interface AttackStrategy
      Parameters:
      unitID - The identifier of the unit performing the attack.
      volume - The audio level for playback.
